channel.title: CPAN Security Group — recent CVEs
channel.description: Most recent CVEs assigned by CPAN Security Group (cpansec).

item.title: CVE-2026-11527 — Config::IniFiles versions before 3.001000 for Perl allow OS command injection and file overwrite via a 2-arg open() of the -file argument in _make_filehandle
item.description: Config::IniFiles versions before 3.001000 for Perl allow OS command injection and file overwrite via a 2-arg open() of the -file argument in _make_filehandle. Config::IniFiles::_make_filehandle opens a filename argument with Perl's 2-arg open(), so a…

item.title: CVE-2026-11526 — GD versions before 2.86 for Perl allow OS command injection and file overwrite via a 2-arg open() of filename arguments in _make_filehandle
item.description: GD versions before 2.86 for Perl allow OS command injection and file overwrite via a 2-arg open() of filename arguments in _make_filehandle. GD::Image::_make_filehandle opens a filename argument with Perl's 2-arg open(), so a filename that begins or…

item.title: CVE-2026-9641 · 5.3 MEDIUM 🌩️ — Crypt::PBKDF2 versions before 0.261630 for Perl have a weak default algorithm and number of iterations
item.description: Crypt::PBKDF2 versions before 0.261630 for Perl have a weak default algorithm and number of iterations. The default algorithm is HMAC-SHA1, which should only be used for legacy systems. These versions default to using 1000 iterations. Depending on th…

item.title: CVE-2026-9638 · 7.5 HIGH 🌩️ — Crypt::PBKDF2 versions before 0.261630 for Perl generate insecure random values for salts
item.description: Crypt::PBKDF2 versions before 0.261630 for Perl generate insecure random values for salts. These versions use the built-in rand function, which is predictable and unsuitable for cryptography.

item.title: CVE-2017-20240 · 5.9 MEDIUM 🌩️ — Crypt::PBKDF2 versions before 0.261630 for Perl are vulnerable to timing attacks
item.description: Crypt::PBKDF2 versions before 0.261630 for Perl are vulnerable to timing attacks. These versions use Perl's built-in eq comparison. Discrepancies in timing could be used to guess the underlying derived-key.

item.title: CVE-2026-50639 · 6.5 MEDIUM 🌩️ — Metrics::Any::Adapter::SignalFx versions before 0.04 for Perl does not protect against metric injections
item.description: Metrics::Any::Adapter::SignalFx versions before 0.04 for Perl does not protect against metric injections. The statsd protocol (and extensions such as dogstatsd) allow mutiple metrics,separated by newlines, to be sent per packet. Metrics::Any::Adapter…

item.title: CVE-2026-50638 · 9.1 CRITICAL 🌩️ — Metrics::Any::Adapter::DogStatsd versions before 0.04 for Perl does not protect against metric injections
item.description: Metrics::Any::Adapter::DogStatsd versions before 0.04 for Perl does not protect against metric injections. The statsd protocol (and extensions such as dogstatsd) allow mutiple metrics,separated by newlines, to be sent per packet. Metrics::Any::Adapte…

item.title: CVE-2026-50637 · 8.2 HIGH 🌩️ — Metrics::Any::Adapter::Statsd versions before 0.04 for Perl does not protect against metric injections
item.description: Metrics::Any::Adapter::Statsd versions before 0.04 for Perl does not protect against metric injections. The statsd protocol (and extensions) allow mutiple metrics,separated by newlines, to be sent per packet. The send method does not validate the con…

item.title: CVE-2009-10007 — Catalyst::Plugin::Authentication versions before 0.10_027 for Perl is susceptible to session fixation attacks
item.description: Catalyst::Plugin::Authentication versions before 0.10_027 for Perl is susceptible to session fixation attacks. Catalyst::Plugin::Authentication does not automatically change the session id after authentication. An attacker that obtains a session id c…

item.title: CVE-2026-9698 · 7.5 HIGH 🌩️ — DBI versions before 1.648 for Perl saved errors in a limited-sized buffer
item.description: DBI versions before 1.648 for Perl saved errors in a limited-sized buffer. Error messages that were returned when RaiseError, PrintError or HandleError were set were written to a 200-byte buffer without a length limit. Attackers that can influence th…

item.title: CVE-2026-10725 · 7.5 HIGH 🌩️ — Protocol::HTTP2 versions before 1.13 for Perl is vulnerable to a HTTP/2 Bomb
item.description: Protocol::HTTP2 versions before 1.13 for Perl is vulnerable to a HTTP/2 Bomb. Protocol::HTTP2's inbound HPACK path has no header-list size limit, so a small HTTP/2 request can expand into large server memory (the "HTTP/2 bomb"). The headers_decode me…

item.title: CVE-2026-11362 · 9.8 CRITICAL 🌩️ — DataDog::DogStatsd versions through 0.07 for Perl allow metric injections from event tags
item.description: DataDog::DogStatsd versions through 0.07 for Perl allow metric injections from event tags. DataDog::DogStatsd does not properly sanitise input, allowing metric injections of data from untrusted sources. The format_event method (used by the event meth…

item.title: CVE-2026-9270 · 9.1 CRITICAL 🌩️ — DataDog::DogStatsd versions through 0.07 for Perl allow metric injections
item.description: DataDog::DogStatsd versions through 0.07 for Perl allow metric injections. DataDog::DogStatsd does not properly sanitise input, allowing metric injections of data from untrusted sources. The send_stats method does not remove newlines from metric name…

item.title: CVE-2026-10879 · 9.8 CRITICAL 🌩️ — DBI versions before 1.648 for Perl have a heap overflow when preparsing SQL statements with more than 9 binders
item.description: DBI versions before 1.648 for Perl have a heap overflow when preparsing SQL statements with more than 9 binders. The preparse method expands SQL placeholder characters to numbered binders of the form :pN, but only allocates three characters per binde…

item.title: CVE-2026-49942 · 7.3 HIGH 🌩️ — Net::CIDR::Set versions through 0.20 for Perl did not validate network masks
item.description: Net::CIDR::Set versions through 0.20 for Perl did not validate network masks. The mask portion of a network mask could contain Unicode digits such as the Arabic-Indic One (U+0661), or non-digits, which were ignored. This could allow network masks to…

item.title: CVE-2026-49941 · 7.5 HIGH 🌩️ — Net::CIDR::Set versions through 0.20 for Perl did not validate IP addresses
item.description: Net::CIDR::Set versions through 0.20 for Perl did not validate IP addresses. The add method called the _encode method to parse addresses. If the addresses did not look like netmasks or network ranges, then they were assumed to single IP addresses and…

item.title: CVE-2026-49940 · 6.5 MEDIUM 🌩️ — Net::CIDR::Set versions through 0.20 for Perl accept non-ASCII IP addresses and netmasks
item.description: Net::CIDR::Set versions through 0.20 for Perl accept non-ASCII IP addresses and netmasks. Unicode digits such as the Arabic-Indic One (U+0661) were accepted but not properly parsed as numbers. This could allow network masks to accept larger networks.

item.title: CVE-2026-46741 · 7.5 HIGH 🌩️ — Etsy::StatsD versions through 1.002002 for Perl allow metric injections
item.description: Etsy::StatsD versions through 1.002002 for Perl allow metric injections. The metric names and values are not checked for newlines, colons or pipes. Metrics generated from untrusted sources could inject additional statsd metrics. Note that the git rep…

item.title: CVE-2026-46739 · 5.3 MEDIUM 🌩️ — Net::Statsd versions before 0.13 for Perl allow metric injections
item.description: Net::Statsd versions before 0.13 for Perl allow metric injections. The metric names are not checked for newlines, colons or pipes. Metrics generated from untrusted sources could inject additional statsd metrics. The update_stats (used for updating co…

item.title: CVE-2026-8829 · 7.5 HIGH 🌩️ — HTML::Entities versions before 3.84 for Perl read freed heap memory in _decode_entities
item.description: HTML::Entities versions before 3.84 for Perl read freed heap memory in _decode_entities. The XS routine backing HTML::Entities::_decode_entities cached a pointer (repl) into the entity-value SV returned by hv_fetch on the entity2char hash. When the i…

item.title: CVE-2026-8722 · 6.5 MEDIUM 🌩️ — Net::Async::Statsd::Client versions through 0.005 for Perl allow metric injections
item.description: Net::Async::Statsd::Client versions through 0.005 for Perl allow metric injections. The metric names are not checked for newlines, colons or pipes. Metrics generated from untrusted sources could inject additional statsd metrics.

item.title: CVE-2026-9516 · 7.5 HIGH 🌩️ — Cpanel::JSON::XS versions before 4.41 for Perl allow denial of service via UTF-8 BOM prefixed input when a decode filter callback throws
item.description: Cpanel::JSON::XS versions before 4.41 for Perl allow denial of service via UTF-8 BOM prefixed input when a decode filter callback throws. To skip a leading 3-byte UTF-8 BOM, decode_json() advances the input scalar's string pointer past the mark with…

item.title: CVE-2026-9334 · 7.3 HIGH 🌩️ — Cpanel::JSON::XS versions before 4.41 for Perl allow type confusion via duplicate object keys when dupkeys_as_arrayref is enabled
item.description: Cpanel::JSON::XS versions before 4.41 for Perl allow type confusion via duplicate object keys when dupkeys_as_arrayref is enabled. decode_hv() collapses duplicate object keys into an array reference under dupkeys_as_arrayref. The branch reached for a…

item.title: CVE-2026-8796 · 8.1 HIGH 🌩️ — Sereal::Decoder versions before 5.005 for Perl allow heap out-of-bounds read via crafted input
item.description: Sereal::Decoder versions before 5.005 for Perl allow heap out-of-bounds read via crafted input. In Perl/Decoder/srl_decoder.c, srl_read_object() and srl_read_hash() process a COPY tag, a back-reference whose target byte the decoder re-decodes as a fr…

item.title: CVE-2026-8594 · 6.2 MEDIUM — Text::LineFold versions through 2019.001 for Perl duplicate the output based on the number of special break characters
item.description: Text::LineFold versions through 2019.001 for Perl duplicate the output based on the number of special break characters. Text::LineFold splits the input string by specific line break characters (such as VT, FF and others) into segments, but applies th…

item.title: CVE-2026-41565 · 7.5 HIGH 🌩️ — CryptX versions before 0.088_001 for Perl have a stack buffer overflow in four AEAD decrypt_verify helpers
item.description: CryptX versions before 0.088_001 for Perl have a stack buffer overflow in four AEAD decrypt_verify helpers. The gcm_decrypt_verify, ccm_decrypt_verify, chacha20poly1305_decrypt_verify and eax_decrypt_verify XS routines copied the caller-supplied auth…

item.title: CVE-2026-9658 · 7.3 HIGH 🌩️ — Plack::Middleware::Security::Common versions before 0.13.1 for Perl did not block header injections in request paths
item.description: Plack::Middleware::Security::Common versions before 0.13.1 for Perl did not block header injections in request paths. The header injection rule was ineffective at blocking header injections in the request paths unless they were double-encoded, for ex…

item.title: CVE-2026-8450 · 9.1 CRITICAL 🌩️ — HTTP::Daemon versions before 6.17 for Perl allow OS command injection via send_file()
item.description: HTTP::Daemon versions before 6.17 for Perl allow OS command injection via send_file(). send_file() opens its string argument with Perl's 2-arg open(). The 2-arg form interprets magic prefixes: '| cmd' and 'cmd |' open a pipe to a subprocess, '> path'…

item.title: CVE-2026-48962 · 7.3 HIGH 🌩️ — IO::Compress versions before 2.220 for Perl can execute arbitrary code in File::GlobMapper via an attacker-controlled output glob
item.description: IO::Compress versions before 2.220 for Perl can execute arbitrary code in File::GlobMapper via an attacker-controlled output glob. _parseOutputGlob() wraps the caller-supplied output glob string in double quotes and stores it in the parser state; _ge…

item.title: CVE-2026-48961 · 7.3 HIGH 🌩️ — IO::Compress versions from 2.207 before 2.220 for Perl ship a zipdetails CLI tool that crashes with undefined subroutine on Info-ZIP Unix Extra Field with 8-byte UID or GID
item.description: IO::Compress versions from 2.207 before 2.220 for Perl ship a zipdetails CLI tool that crashes with undefined subroutine on Info-ZIP Unix Extra Field with 8-byte UID or GID. When decode_ux() in bin/zipdetails handles an Info-ZIP Unix Extra Field (tag…

item.title: CVE-2026-48959 · 7.5 HIGH 🌩️ — IO::Uncompress::Unzip versions before 2.220 for Perl allow CPU exhaustion via per-byte read loop in fastForward
item.description: IO::Uncompress::Unzip versions before 2.220 for Perl allow CPU exhaustion via per-byte read loop in fastForward. fastForward() compares length $offset (the digit count of the offset, 1 to 19) against the chunk size $c instead of $offset itself, so $c…

item.title: CVE-2025-15649 · 5.5 MEDIUM — IO::Uncompress::Unzip versions before 2.215 for Perl propagate uncaught exception when parsing zip header with malformed DOS date
item.description: IO::Uncompress::Unzip versions before 2.215 for Perl propagate uncaught exception when parsing zip header with malformed DOS date. _dosToUnixTime() decodes the local-file-header last-modification date field and calls Time::Local::timelocal() without…

item.title: CVE-2026-8647 · 4.8 MEDIUM 🌩️ — Crypt::ScryptKDF versions through 0.010 for Perl uses insecure random number source when no CSPRNG module is available
item.description: Crypt::ScryptKDF versions through 0.010 for Perl uses insecure random number source when no CSPRNG module is available. The random_bytes function fell back to using the built-in rand() function when none of the Perl modules Crypt::PRNG, Crypt::OpenSS…

item.title: CVE-2026-46740 · 5.3 MEDIUM 🌩️ — Mojolicious::Plugin::Statsd versions through 0.04 for Perl allowed metric injections
item.description: Mojolicious::Plugin::Statsd versions through 0.04 for Perl allowed metric injections. The metric names and set values were not checked for newlines, colons or pipes. Metrics generated from untrusted sources could inject additional statsd metrics. Ver…

item.title: CVE-2026-9538 · 7.5 HIGH 🌩️ — Archive::Tar versions before 3.10 for Perl allow memory exhaustion via attacker controlled entry size field in tar header
item.description: Archive::Tar versions before 3.10 for Perl allow memory exhaustion via attacker controlled entry size field in tar header. _read_tar() reads each entry's payload with $handle->read($$data, $block), where $block is derived from the entry's 12-byte siz…

item.title: CVE-2026-42497 · 7.5 HIGH 🌩️ — Archive::Tar versions before 3.08 for Perl extract hardlinks to attacker controlled paths outside the extraction directory
item.description: Archive::Tar versions before 3.08 for Perl extract hardlinks to attacker controlled paths outside the extraction directory. _make_special_file() passes the tar header's linkname to link() without validating it against absolute paths or .. segments, c…

item.title: CVE-2026-42496 · 9.1 CRITICAL 🌩️ — Archive::Tar versions before 3.08 for Perl extract symlinks with attacker controlled targets outside the extraction directory
item.description: Archive::Tar versions before 3.08 for Perl extract symlinks with attacker controlled targets outside the extraction directory. _make_special_file() passes the tar header's linkname to symlink() without validating it against absolute paths or .. segme…

item.title: CVE-2026-8376 · 7.3 HIGH 🌩️ — Perl versions through 5.43.10 have a heap buffer overflow when compiling regular expressions with a repeated fixed string on 32-bit builds
item.description: Perl versions through 5.43.10 have a heap buffer overflow when compiling regular expressions with a repeated fixed string on 32-bit builds. Perl_study_chunk in regcomp_study.c checked the size of the joined substring buffer in characters rather than…

item.title: CVE-2026-5091 · 5.1 MEDIUM — Catalyst::Plugin::Authentication versions through 0.10024 for Perl is susceptible to timing attacks
item.description: Catalyst::Plugin::Authentication versions through 0.10024 for Perl is susceptible to timing attacks. These versions use Perl's built-in eq comparison. Discrepencies in timing could be used to guess the underlying hash or password.

item.title: CVE-2026-46473 · 7.5 HIGH 🌩️ — Authen::TOTP versions before 0.1.1 for Perl generate secrets using rand
item.description: Authen::TOTP versions before 0.1.1 for Perl generate secrets using rand. Secrets were generated using Perl's built-in rand function, which is predictable and unsuitable for security usage.

item.title: CVE-2026-47372 · 9.1 CRITICAL 🌩️ — Crypt::SaltedHash versions through 0.09 for Perl generate insecure random values for salts
item.description: Crypt::SaltedHash versions through 0.09 for Perl generate insecure random values for salts. These versions use the built-in rand function, which is predictable and unsuitable for cryptography.

item.title: CVE-2026-47373 · 7.5 HIGH 🌩️ — Crypt::SaltedHash versions through 0.09 for Perl is susceptible to timing attacks
item.description: Crypt::SaltedHash versions through 0.09 for Perl is susceptible to timing attacks. These versions use Perl's built-in eq comparison. Discrepencies in timing could be used to guess the underlying hash.

item.title: CVE-2026-5090 · 6.1 MEDIUM 🌩️ — Template::Plugin::HTML versions through 3.102 for Perl allows HTML and JavaScript to be injected
item.description: Template::Plugin::HTML versions through 3.102 for Perl allows HTML and JavaScript to be injected. The html_filter function did not escape single quotes. HTML attributes inside of single quotes could be have code injected. For example, the variable "v…

item.title: CVE-2026-8788 · 7.3 HIGH 🌩️ — Net::Statsd::Lite versions through 0.10.0 for Perl allowed metric injections
item.description: Net::Statsd::Lite versions through 0.10.0 for Perl allowed metric injections. The values from the set_add method were not checked for newlines, colons or pipes. Metrics generated from untrusted sources could inject additional statsd metrics. Note tha…

item.title: CVE-2026-8721 · 9.8 CRITICAL 🌩️ — Crypt::OpenSSL::PKCS12 versions through 1.94 for Perl truncates passwords with embedded NULLs
item.description: Crypt::OpenSSL::PKCS12 versions through 1.94 for Perl truncates passwords with embedded NULLs. Password parameters in PKCS12.xs are declared char *, which routes through Perl's default typemap to SvPV_nolen. The Perl length is discarded. The C code (…

item.title: CVE-2026-8507 · 9.8 CRITICAL 🌩️ — Crypt::OpenSSL::PKCS12 versions through 1.94 for Perl have out-of-bounds (OOB) write flaws
item.description: Crypt::OpenSSL::PKCS12 versions through 1.94 for Perl have out-of-bounds (OOB) write flaws. When parsing a PKCS12 file, with a >= 1 GiB OCTET STRING (or BIT STRING) attribute on a SAFEBAG, via info() or info_as_hash(), a heap out-of-bounds write woul…

item.title: CVE-2026-46720 · 8.2 HIGH 🌩️ — Net::Statsd::Tiny versions before 0.3.8 for Perl allowed metric injections
item.description: Net::Statsd::Tiny versions before 0.3.8 for Perl allowed metric injections. The metric names and set values were not checked for newlines, colons or pipes. Metrics generated from untrusted sources could inject additional statsd metrics.

item.title: CVE-2026-46719 · 6.5 MEDIUM 🌩️ — Net::Statsd::Lite versions before 0.9.0 for Perl allowed metric injections
item.description: Net::Statsd::Lite versions before 0.9.0 for Perl allowed metric injections. The metric names were not checked for newlines, colons or pipes. Metrics generated from untrusted sources could inject additional statsd metrics.

item.title: CVE-2026-8704 · 6.5 MEDIUM 🌩️ — Crypt::DSA versions through 1.19 for Perl use 2-args open, allowing existing files to be modified
item.description: Crypt::DSA versions through 1.19 for Perl use 2-args open, allowing existing files to be modified.

item.title: CVE-2026-8700 · 7.3 HIGH 🌩️ — Crypt::DSA versions before 1.20 for Perl generate seeds using rand
item.description: Crypt::DSA versions before 1.20 for Perl generate seeds using rand. Seeds were generated using Perl's built-in rand function, which is predictable and unsuitable for security usage.
